title: Differential Equations
subject: Mathematics
content:
# Differential Equations

## Quick Synopsis

Differential equations relate a function to its derivatives. JEE focuses on formation and solving first-order first-degree equations.

## Must Know

- Order is highest derivative order; degree is power of highest derivative after removing radicals/fractions in derivatives.
- Formation of differential equation eliminates arbitrary constants.
- Variable separable equations can be rearranged as f(y)dy = g(x)dx.
- Homogeneous equations use y = vx or x = vy substitution.
- Linear differential equation has form dy/dx + Py = Q.
- Solution includes arbitrary constant for first-order equations.

## Formulas / Rules

- Linear DE integrating factor: IF = e^(∫P dx).
- Solution: y(IF) = ∫Q(IF) dx + C.
- Variable separable: ∫f(y)dy = ∫g(x)dx + C.
- Homogeneous substitution: y = vx, dy/dx = v + x dv/dx.

## JEE Focus

- Identify type before solving.
- For orthogonal trajectories, replace slope m by -1/m.
- Eliminate constants by differentiating enough times.
- Check whether equation is linear in y or x; sometimes dx/dy form is easier.

## Common Traps

- Degree is undefined if equation is not polynomial in derivatives.
- Do not apply linear form when P and Q are not functions of independent variable only.
- Homogeneous in DE means same degree in x,y, not same as homogeneous linear equation.
- For family of curves with two constants, differentiate twice.

## 10-Minute Revision Checklist

- Can you find order and degree?
- Can you form DE from a family?
- Can you solve variable separable equations?
- Can you solve linear first-order DE?
